project.name: Codex-Cultivation
project.description: Codex Cultivation transforms Codex Desktop into a local cultivation-themed growth system. The current Windows implementation uses a reversible Chromium DevTools Protocol injection layer without modifying the official application package, signatures, account data, tasks, or plugin data.

nodes.name: cultivation-base.css
nodes.summary: Defines the foundational Codex Desktop reskin: light and dark design tokens, native shell/sidebar/composer overrides, route-level background art, and responsive readability behavior. It coordinates a single immersive image across home and task surfaces while preserving native interactive controls.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses modern CSS color-mix/oklch tokens, highly specific selectors targeting Codex shell structure, route-aware art treatments, responsive breakpoints, and reduced-motion fallbacks.

nodes.name: cultivation-skin.css
nodes.summary: Builds the cultivation-specific interface layer on top of the base theme, styling the progression HUD, modal tabs and statistics, settings controls, companion and status rail, weekly activity, event history, and the reworked home workspace. It maps cultivation colors and materials onto Codex's native theme tokens for both light and dark modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines native Codex color tokens with custom cultivation variables, attribute-driven component states, generated visual meters, multiple responsive layouts, and explicit motion-reduction handling.

nodes.name: theme.json
nodes.summary: Declares the default '炼气云台' cultivation preset, including localized branding copy, project-picker labels, status text, background image, automatic appearance selection, and image focus/safe-area metadata. The art metadata places the focal point to the right and reserves the left side for readable interface content.
nodes.languageNotes: A versioned JSON preset separates presentation copy and image composition metadata from the CSS implementation.

nodes.name: cultivation-art-prompts.json
nodes.summary: Catalogs reproducible image-generation specifications for realm-specific light and dark wallpapers, persistent male and female companion portraits, and circular function-card artwork. Each asset definition fixes model, dimensions, quality, composition, palette, and strict no-UI/no-text constraints.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array entries act as declarative generation recipes keyed by stable asset IDs and output filenames.

nodes.name: cultivation-design-system.md
nodes.summary: Defines the cultivation workspace visual hierarchy, three-column responsive layout, light and dark material palettes, interaction requirements, and data-integrity rules. It explicitly preserves Codex theme ownership and native controls while constraining accessibility, motion, and estimated-data labeling.

nodes.name: cultivation-system.md
nodes.summary: Specifies the cultivation progression model, including local Token estimation, spirit-stone conversion, realm thresholds, enlightenment rewards, three-day tribulations, theme asset fallback, companion identity rules, and the regression-only debug API. It is the central product-rules reference for renderer state and user-visible progression behavior.

nodes.name: qa-inventory.md
nodes.summary: Provides the acceptance inventory for user-visible behavior, native-control preservation, reversible injection, configuration safety, theme validation, responsive visuals, hostile runtime scenarios, and automated regression coverage. It separates functional, visual, exploratory, automated, and live-Windows signoff responsibilities.

nodes.name: runtime-notes.md
nodes.summary: Documents the hardened Windows runtime contract for discovering and launching the signed Store Codex package, validating loopback CDP identity, supervising reinjection, recording process state, restoring safely, and serializing operations. It also records Node requirements, port selection, image and path defenses, strict TOML handling, and Store-update resilience.

nodes.name: common-windows.ps1
nodes.summary: Shared Windows runtime and safety layer for Codex Cultivation. It validates the official Store package, binds CDP endpoints to the correct Codex process, manages session state, and performs identity-checked shutdowns.
nodes.languageNotes: PowerShell functions use strict path, package, PID, command-line, browser-ID, and loopback-listener checks before mutating processes or state.

nodes.name: Enter-CultivationOperationLock
nodes.summary: Acquires a per-user, non-blocking mutex so install, start, restore, and verification operations cannot overlap.

nodes.name: Test-CultivationPathWithin
nodes.summary: Checks whether a resolved path is a descendant of a trusted root using ordinal case-insensitive Windows path comparison.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationProcessExecutablePath
nodes.summary: Resolves a process executable from CIM metadata or the live process object, returning null when inspection is unavailable.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationNodeRuntime
nodes.summary: Locates Node.js, validates its executable path and version, and enforces the minimum major version required by the injector.

nodes.name: ConvertTo-CultivationCodexInstall
nodes.summary: Converts an AppX package record into a trusted Codex installation descriptor only when Store identity and executable layout checks pass.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationCodexStatePathCandidate
nodes.summary: Builds a limited Codex installation candidate from saved state after confirming the recorded executable matches the expected package layout.

nodes.name: Resolve-CultivationCodexInstallFromState
nodes.summary: Revalidates a saved Codex path and optional package identities against currently registered Store installations before accepting it.

nodes.name: Test-CultivationWebSocketUrl
nodes.summary: Validates that a CDP WebSocket URL is loopback-only, uses the expected port, and has a constrained DevTools path without credentials or query data.

nodes.name: Test-CultivationCdpPageTarget
nodes.summary: Validates an app:// CDP page target and ensures its target ID matches the path embedded in its WebSocket debugger URL.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationCdpTargets
nodes.summary: Queries the local CDP target list and returns only page targets that pass the strict Cultivation target validator.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationCdpBrowserIdentity
nodes.summary: Queries CDP version metadata and extracts a validated browser ID and loopback WebSocket endpoint.

nodes.name: Test-CultivationCodexPortOwner
nodes.summary: Confirms every listener on a candidate port is loopback-bound and owned by the expected official Codex executable.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationVerifiedCdpIdentity
nodes.summary: Combines listener ownership, browser metadata, and page-target checks into a verified CDP session identity.

nodes.name: Read-CultivationState
nodes.summary: Reads and validates persisted session JSON across supported schema versions, rejecting malformed platform, port, PID, and browser identity fields.

nodes.name: Stop-CultivationRecordedInjector
nodes.summary: Stops the saved Node injector only after matching its executable, command line, port, browser ID, and process start time to recorded state.

nodes.name: Stop-CultivationCodex
nodes.summary: Gracefully closes the verified Codex process set, optionally escalating to forced termination only after rechecking executable identity.

nodes.name: config-utf8.ps1
nodes.summary: Provides strict UTF-8, atomic file-update, and constrained TOML editing primitives for installing and restoring the Codex base theme. It preserves backups, detects concurrent changes, and refuses ambiguous TOML shapes.
nodes.languageNotes: The script deliberately avoids a general TOML serializer: it performs guarded line-level edits to preserve unrelated user configuration and encoding.

nodes.name: ConvertFrom-CultivationUtf8Bytes
nodes.summary: Decodes optional-BOM UTF-8 bytes with strict error handling and rejects NUL-bearing content that may be misidentified UTF-16.

nodes.name: Test-CultivationBytesEqual
nodes.summary: Performs an exact byte-by-byte comparison used for optimistic concurrency checks on configuration files.

nodes.name: Assert-CultivationFileUnchanged
nodes.summary: Verifies that a target file still matches the bytes observed earlier, preventing overwrite when another writer changed or created it.

nodes.name: Read-CultivationUtf8File
nodes.summary: Reads a file as raw bytes and decodes it through the strict Cultivation UTF-8 validator.

nodes.name: Write-CultivationUtf8FileAtomically
nodes.summary: Encodes text as UTF-8 without BOM and delegates to the atomic byte writer, optionally enforcing expected prior content.

nodes.name: Write-CultivationBytesAtomically
nodes.summary: Writes through a temporary file and atomic replacement while supporting compare-before-swap semantics and cleaning temporary artifacts.

nodes.name: ConvertTo-CultivationTomlAsciiEscapeProbe
nodes.summary: Normalizes TOML Unicode escapes for ASCII key characters in a probe copy so equivalent escaped keys can be detected safely.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationTomlArrayBracketBalance
nodes.summary: Counts TOML array brackets outside quotes and comments to identify unsupported multiline arrays.

nodes.name: Assert-CultivationTomlLineEditingSafe
nodes.summary: Rejects multiline strings, multiline arrays, and escaped desktop-key aliases that would make line-oriented TOML editing unsafe.

nodes.name: Assert-CultivationDesktopShapeSupported
nodes.summary: Ensures the Codex desktop configuration uses one supported table shape and contains no ambiguous dotted, inline, array, or escaped managed keys.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationDesktopSection
nodes.summary: Locates the supported desktop TOML section and returns exact body and section offsets for minimal string replacement.

nodes.name: Add-CultivationDesktopSection
nodes.summary: Appends a desktop TOML section while preserving the source file's newline convention and spacing.

nodes.name: Set-CultivationSectionSetting
nodes.summary: Adds, replaces, or removes one setting in a TOML section while rejecting duplicate keys and preserving newline style.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationSectionSettingLine
nodes.summary: Retrieves a single exact setting line from a TOML section and refuses ambiguous duplicate definitions.

nodes.name: Test-CultivationLegacyManagedLightTrio
nodes.summary: Detects the exact legacy three-setting theme signature so older installations can be migrated without overwriting user appearance choices.

nodes.name: Read-CultivationAppearanceMarker
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the sidecar marker that records ownership boundaries for appearance settings.

nodes.name: Write-CultivationAppearanceMarker
nodes.summary: Writes a versioned appearance ownership marker through the guarded UTF-8 atomic writer.

nodes.name: Install-CultivationBaseTheme
nodes.summary: Backs up Codex configuration and transactionally injects only the managed light code and chrome theme settings, including legacy migration handling.

nodes.name: Restore-CultivationBaseTheme
nodes.summary: Restores managed theme keys from the pre-install backup while preserving unrelated configuration changes made after installation.

nodes.name: Restore-CultivationConfigBackup
nodes.summary: Restores the exact pre-install configuration bytes and first preserves the current config as a timestamped recovery backup.

nodes.name: Archive-CultivationConfigBackup
nodes.summary: Moves a completed pre-install backup to an archive path and removes its appearance ownership marker.

nodes.name: install-codex-cultivation.ps1
nodes.summary: Installation entry point that validates prerequisites, initializes the theme store, patches the Codex base theme, and optionally creates desktop and Start Menu shortcuts plus the tray controller.

nodes.name: request-cultivation-art.ps1
nodes.summary: Development utility that submits a configured cultivation-art job to an image generation API, downloads the HTTPS result, validates and center-crops it, and atomically promotes a normalized PNG asset.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses .NET HttpClient and System.Drawing with explicit disposal, image dimension limits, and preservation of usable rejected downloads.

nodes.name: restore-codex-cultivation.ps1
nodes.summary: Reversible teardown entry point that safely closes the tray, injector, and verified Codex CDP session, selectively restores or fully recovers configuration, removes shortcuts on uninstall, and optionally relaunches Codex.

nodes.name: Stop-CultivationTrayProcess
nodes.summary: Finds PowerShell processes whose command line references the exact tray script path and terminates only those matching tray instances.

nodes.name: start-codex-cultivation.ps1
nodes.summary: Primary launcher that verifies the Store Codex identity, establishes a loopback-only CDP session, starts the Node injector watcher, records schema-versioned state, verifies the live skin, and rolls back failures.
nodes.languageNotes: The startup transaction preserves cancellation side-effect freedom and revalidates process, port, package, and browser identity before each sensitive action.

nodes.name: theme-windows.ps1
nodes.summary: Secure Windows theme-store implementation for active, saved, generated, and paused theme state. It validates image size and metadata, prevents path traversal and reparse-point escapes, and manages theme JSON plus image copies.

nodes.name: Assert-CultivationNoReparseComponents
nodes.summary: Walks every existing component of a managed path and rejects junctions or symbolic links that could redirect writes outside the state area.

nodes.name: Ensure-CultivationManagedDirectory
nodes.summary: Creates a directory only when it stays within the declared state root and passes reparse-point checks before and after creation.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationValidatedImageMetadata
nodes.summary: Runs the Node image metadata helper and validates its JSON result against dimension and pixel-count safety limits.

nodes.name: Assert-CultivationImageFile
nodes.summary: Validates theme image existence, extension, non-empty size, 16 MB limit, and optionally decoded metadata.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationThemePaths
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical active, saved, image archive, pause marker, and state paths beneath the Cultivation state root.

nodes.name: Test-CultivationThemePathWithin
nodes.summary: Confirms a theme path is inside its root and that every existing component is free of reparse points.

nodes.name: Read-CultivationTheme
nodes.summary: Loads theme metadata, enforces a relative image reference contained in the theme directory, and validates the referenced image.

nodes.name: Write-CultivationTheme
nodes.summary: Serializes theme metadata and atomically writes theme.json after reparse-point checks on the directory and target.

nodes.name: Initialize-CultivationThemeStore
nodes.summary: Creates the managed theme directories, installs default active and preset assets when missing, validates all copied images, and verifies the active theme.

nodes.name: Set-CultivationActiveTheme
nodes.summary: Validates and stages a selected image, updates active theme metadata, removes the superseded managed image, and archives the new artwork.

nodes.name: Save-CultivationCurrentTheme
nodes.summary: Copies the active image and cloned metadata into a uniquely named saved-theme directory after validating the user-visible theme name.

nodes.name: Get-CultivationSavedThemes
nodes.summary: Enumerates valid saved theme directories, suppresses invalid entries, and returns display metadata sorted by name.

nodes.name: Use-CultivationSavedTheme
nodes.summary: Validates that a chosen theme belongs to the saved-theme root, loads it, and promotes a cloned copy to the active theme.

nodes.name: Set-CultivationPaused
nodes.summary: Creates or removes the managed pause marker used by the injector watcher to suspend skin application.

nodes.name: tray-codex-cultivation.ps1
nodes.summary: Single-instance Windows Forms tray controller that displays live Cultivation status, applies or pauses the skin, selects and saves themes, opens assets, and launches restore workflows.

nodes.name: Add-CultivationTrayItem
nodes.summary: Creates a tray menu item, binds a closure-safe click handler, and routes action failures to the tray error dialog.

nodes.name: Rebuild-CultivationTrayMenu
nodes.summary: Reconstructs the dynamic tray menu from current pause, state, active-theme, and saved-theme data and binds all control actions.

nodes.name: verify-codex-cultivation.ps1
nodes.summary: Verification entry point that resolves the active or saved Codex package identity, validates the loopback CDP browser against session state, and delegates visual/runtime verification to the Node injector.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Defines the project-local exclusion patterns used by Understand Anything scanning; the current file keeps suggested exclusions commented so the full repository remains analyzable.

nodes.name: NOTICE.md
nodes.summary: Records project independence, attribution to the reversible injection ideas derived from Codex Dream Skin, licensing limitations, asset-rights responsibilities, and trademark notices.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Introduces Codex Cultivation as a reversible Windows-first Codex Desktop customization, covering features, installation, verification, restoration, local data semantics, art generation, security boundaries, and tests.

nodes.name: openai.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines the Codex skill interface metadata, including its display name, short description, and default prompt for invoking the cultivation workflow.

nodes.name: renderer-inject.js
nodes.summary: Implements the renderer-side cultivation experience: adaptive artwork analysis, local cultivation progression and persistence, responsive DOM augmentation, native Codex action binding, event handling, cleanup, and a verification debug API.
nodes.languageNotes: A large browser-side JavaScript IIFE uses placeholder substitution to embed CSS, theme metadata, and image data before CDP evaluation.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Summarizes the 1.8.0 release, including the responsive cultivation home, progression mechanics, charts, paired themes, companion assets, image workflow, and retained CDP safety controls.

nodes.name: image-metadata.mjs
nodes.summary: Parses PNG, JPEG, and WebP headers without decoding pixels, validates strict dimension and pixel-count limits, classifies aspect behavior, and exposes a read-only CLI used by theme safety checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses bounded binary-header parsing for three image formats and rejects unsafe dimensions before payload construction.

nodes.name: injector.mjs
nodes.summary: Builds validated theme payloads and manages reversible Codex renderer injection over loopback CDP, with browser identity checks, early-document bootstrap, watch and one-shot modes, verification, screenshots, and cleanup.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ESM, WebSocket CDP sessions, SHA-256 theme fingerprints, strict filesystem containment checks, and top-level await in its CLI dispatch.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the codex-cultivation skill workflow, guardrails, and important implementation files for safe installation, verification, updating, and restoration on Windows.

nodes.name: image-metadata.test.mjs
nodes.summary: Regression tests image header parsing, CLI output, aspect classification, dimension limits, and malformed or oversized image rejection.

nodes.name: injector-bootstrap.test.mjs
nodes.summary: Exercises the early injection bootstrap in a VM fixture, verifying Codex shell guards, generation replacement, registration ordering, fallback scoping, and cleanup of new-document scripts.

nodes.name: injector-one-shot.test.mjs
nodes.summary: Runs the injector against a local fake CDP endpoint to ensure one-shot, verify, and remove modes validate Browser ID through the version endpoint before timing out safely.

nodes.name: renderer-inject.test.mjs
nodes.summary: Provides a comprehensive VM-based DOM fixture that verifies adaptive renderer installation and cleanup, auxiliary-window safety, theme and artwork classification, reinjection behavior, and cultivation state transitions.

nodes.name: run-tests.ps1
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the Windows regression suite for UTF-8 config transactions, scoped restoration, CDP and Store identity validation, theme-store containment, image limits, tray/startup behavior, payload checks, and JavaScript tests.
nodes.languageNotes: A procedural PowerShell test harness dot-sources production scripts and validates both behavior and required safety invariants.

nodes.name: jpegDimensions
nodes.summary: Walks JPEG markers until a supported start-of-frame segment is found, then returns validated positive width and height values.

nodes.name: webpDimensions
nodes.summary: Parses RIFF WebP containers and extracts dimensions from VP8X, VP8L, or VP8 payload variants while respecting declared chunk bounds and padding.

nodes.name: classifyImageDimensions
nodes.summary: Rejects non-integer, oversized, or excessive-pixel dimensions and maps safe aspect ratios to layout categories and task artwork modes.

nodes.name: readImageMetadata
nodes.summary: Selects the appropriate PNG, JPEG, or WebP header parser from extension or magic bytes and returns only safely classified metadata.

nodes.name: CdpSession
nodes.summary: Encapsulates a validated CDP WebSocket connection, request correlation, event dispatch, JavaScript evaluation, timeouts, and deterministic socket cleanup.

nodes.name: BrowserIdentityAnchor
nodes.summary: Maintains a browser-level CDP connection that detects closure and anchors the injector to the expected browser identity during watch mode.

nodes.name: parseArgs
nodes.summary: Parses injector CLI modes and paths, then enforces safe port, timeout, Browser ID, and mode-specific argument constraints.

nodes.name: isValidCdpPageTarget
nodes.summary: Accepts only app-protocol page targets whose ID and loopback debugger path exactly correspond.

nodes.name: fetchCdpJson
nodes.summary: Fetches a loopback CDP JSON resource with timeout handling and validates successful JSON responses.

nodes.name: listAppTargets
nodes.summary: Validates the live browser identity from the CDP version endpoint and returns only acceptable Codex app page targets.

nodes.name: connectBrowserIdentityAnchor
nodes.summary: Opens and validates the browser-level identity anchor used to fail closed when the watched Codex browser instance changes.

nodes.name: loadTheme
nodes.summary: Loads theme metadata and artwork through real paths, enforces containment, format, size, color, and dimension rules, and computes a fingerprint and source stamp.

nodes.name: loadPayload
nodes.summary: Combines base and cultivation CSS, renderer code, validated theme art, and optional realm, companion, and card assets into a self-contained renderer payload.

nodes.name: probeSession
nodes.summary: Evaluates stable shell markers in a renderer and identifies whether the target is a usable Codex surface.

nodes.name: waitForCodexProbe
nodes.summary: Polls a renderer through document transitions until Codex shell markers appear or the short bootstrap timeout expires.

nodes.name: connectCodexTargets
nodes.summary: Repeatedly discovers targets, opens sessions, rejects non-Codex surfaces, and returns verified renderer connections within a bounded timeout.

nodes.name: earlyPayloadFor
nodes.summary: Generates a generation-scoped new-document bootstrap that waits for the main Codex shell before installing the payload and self-terminates after ten seconds.

nodes.name: removeFromSession
nodes.summary: Disables cultivation injection and removes state, classes, CSS variables, route markers, and injected style or chrome nodes from a renderer.

nodes.name: verifyRemovedSession
nodes.summary: Confirms that cultivation state, DOM markers, styles, and injected nodes are absent after restoration.

nodes.name: verifySession
nodes.summary: Collects installation, version, DOM geometry, pointer-event, card-count, and overflow evidence and determines whether the renderer is safely usable.

nodes.name: waitForVerifiedSession
nodes.summary: Polls renderer verification until it passes or a bounded timeout returns the latest diagnostic result.

nodes.name: capture
nodes.summary: Prepares the renderer by dismissing overlays and moving the pointer, captures a viewport PNG through CDP, and writes it to the requested path.

nodes.name: runOneShot
nodes.summary: Executes once, verify, remove, reload, or screenshot operations across all verified Codex renderers and reports per-target results with failure exit status.

nodes.name: runWatch
nodes.summary: Continuously audits theme changes and Codex targets, manages early injection and load fallbacks, reapplies revisions, handles pause and identity failures, and cleans all sessions on exit.

nodes.name: createFixture
nodes.summary: Builds a minimal VM window, document, mutation observer, and timer fixture for deterministic early-bootstrap testing.

nodes.name: runMode
nodes.summary: Spawns the injector in a selected one-shot mode, captures output, and resolves with its exit code and combined diagnostics.

nodes.name: createFixture
nodes.summary: Constructs an extensive VM DOM and browser API fixture with class mutation tracking, route nodes, local storage, images, timers, and observers for renderer regression tests.

layers.name: Renderer Experience
layers.description: The injected renderer, design-token foundation, and cultivation skin that augment Codex Desktop with responsive progression UI while retaining native controls.

layers.name: Windows Runtime and Lifecycle
layers.description: PowerShell lifecycle entry points and the Node CDP service that install, launch, supervise, verify, configure, and reversibly remove the Codex Cultivation session.

layers.name: Theme and Asset Pipeline
layers.description: Theme presets, reproducible art specifications, image generation, and bounded image metadata validation used to produce safe cultivation artwork payloads.

layers.name: Regression and Integration Tests
layers.description: VM fixtures, fake-CDP integration checks, image parser tests, and the PowerShell suite runner that protect injection, identity, state, and rollback behavior.

layers.name: System and Quality Documentation
layers.description: Authoritative references for progression rules, visual design, hardened Windows runtime behavior, and acceptance coverage across the cultivation experience.

layers.name: Project and Skill Interface
layers.description: User-facing project guidance, release and legal records, Codex skill invocation metadata, workflow guardrails, and local analysis-scope configuration.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand Codex Cultivation as a reversible, Windows-first customization of Codex Desktop rather than a modified application build. It introduces the feature set, installation path, local-state model, security boundary, verification workflow, and full restoration promise that frame every later step.

tour.title: Progression Rules
tour.description: With the project goal established, read the central product specification that defines how local Token estimates become spirit stones, realms, enlightenment rewards, and tribulation events. These rules explain what the renderer must persist and display, while also documenting fallback behavior, companion identity, and the regression-only debug surface.

tour.title: Safe Session Startup
tour.description: The user-facing start script turns the product model into a live session: it verifies the official Store Codex identity, opens a loopback-only CDP endpoint, launches the injector, records state, and rolls back failed starts. Its shared Windows module supplies the safety primitives for package discovery, process-to-endpoint binding, session state, and identity-checked shutdown, so the launcher never treats an arbitrary Chromium process as Codex.
tour.languageLesson: PowerShell scripts can share functions through dot-sourcing, but lifecycle automation still needs explicit process identity checks and structured state because process names and ports alone are not trustworthy identifiers.

tour.title: CDP Injection Runtime
tour.description: The Node injector is the architectural hub and the broadest-scope module in the graph. Building on the verified session from Step 3, it validates theme inputs, connects through loopback Chromium DevTools Protocol, installs an early-document bootstrap, supports watch and one-shot modes, verifies the result, and cleans up reversibly.
tour.languageLesson: An ES module runtime can use CDP as a remote-control protocol for an existing Chromium renderer; early-document scripts are registered before page code runs so reinjection survives navigation and renderer recreation.

tour.title: Renderer Experience
tour.description: Once the injector reaches a Codex renderer, this payload creates the actual cultivation experience inside the page. It analyzes artwork, maintains local progression state, augments the DOM responsively, binds new controls back to native Codex actions, handles events and cleanup, and exposes a narrow verification API for tests.
tour.languageLesson: DOM augmentation against an application you do not own should be idempotent: injected elements, listeners, and observers need stable markers and cleanup paths so repeated injection does not duplicate behavior.

tour.title: Theme Composition
tour.description: The visual layer is split between a base reskin that adapts Codex's native shell and a cultivation skin that styles progression-specific HUDs, dialogs, statistics, companions, and activity views. The versioned theme preset keeps localized copy, appearance selection, background choice, focal point, and safe-area metadata outside the CSS and renderer logic, making visual variants configurable without changing the runtime architecture.
tour.languageLesson: CSS custom properties and JSON presets form a useful separation of concerns: CSS defines reusable rendering rules, while data-driven presets select copy, assets, colors, and composition metadata.

tour.title: Control and Verification
tour.description: The tray controller provides the ongoing user interface for inspecting status, applying or pausing the skin, selecting themes, opening assets, and initiating restoration. The verification entry point independently revalidates the saved package and loopback CDP identity before asking the injector to check the live renderer, so a visible-looking result is not accepted without runtime evidence.

tour.title: Reversible Teardown
tour.description: Restoration is a first-class lifecycle path rather than an afterthought: the restore script closes only the verified tray, injector, and Codex CDP session, recovers configuration at the requested scope, and can remove shortcuts or relaunch Codex. The runtime contract documents the invariants behind this behavior, including signed Store-package discovery, loopback identity, serialized operations, strict TOML updates, bounded paths and images, and resilience to Store updates.

tour.title: Regression Safety Net
tour.description: The PowerShell suite runner exercises the failure-prone boundaries introduced in Steps 3-8: atomic UTF-8 configuration changes, scoped restoration, Store and CDP identity, theme containment, image limits, startup and tray behavior, renderer payloads, and JavaScript integration tests. The QA inventory then separates automated coverage from functional, visual, exploratory, hostile-runtime, and live-Windows signoff, showing where human acceptance is still required.

tour.title: Skill Workflow
tour.description: Finish with the skill-facing documentation that turns the implementation into a repeatable Codex workflow. It identifies the important files and guardrails for installation, verification, updates, and restoration, providing an operational map back across the launcher, injector, renderer, theme, and safety mechanisms covered in the tour.
